fresh_facts.claim: USS Boxer amphibious ready group deployed to the Middle East from San Diego on March 18, 2026.
Pentagon is expected to deploy 3,000 to 4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division from Fort Bragg, North Carolina to the Middle East.
USS Tripoli amphibious ready group was positioned at Naval Support Facility Diego Garcia on March 23, 2026, carrying approximately 3,500 Marines en route to the Middle East.
Three amphibious ready groups with Marine expeditionary units represent realization of Marine Corps Commandant General Eric Smith's plan for three forward-postured ARG/MEUs.
Pentagon is preparing for possible ground operations in Iran as of March 29, 2026, though White House stated no final decision has been made.
Over 50,000 US forces are deployed in the Middle East as of March 29, 2026, approximately 10,000 above normal deployment levels.
Current US troop levels in the Middle East are sufficient for brief targeted missions around the Strait of Hormuz but insufficient for a full-scale invasion of Iran.
Trump administration has discussed options including securing the Strait of Hormuz by deploying forces to Iran's shoreline and sending ground forces to Kharg Island.
Kharg Island handles 90% of Iran's oil exports.
US has carried out strikes against approximately 9,000 targets inside Iran since February 28, 2026.
As of March 29, 2026, 13 US troops have been killed and 290 have been wounded in the conflict, with 255 having returned to duty.
Israel continues intensifying strikes on nuclear and military targets inside Iran as of March 29, 2026, with sirens sounding across central Israel and missile barrages reported in the north.
President Trump announced a 10-day suspension of strikes on Iranian power plants until April 6, 2026, 8 PM ET, following an Iranian request, while continuing operations elsewhere.
Trump expressed doubt on an Iran peace deal, stating it depends on Iranian leadership convincing him to halt the war.
Post-Ali Khamenei's death, the clerical establishment struggles for survival amid competing narratives on Iran's future.
Maryam Rajavi's Ten-Point Plan, endorsed March 15, 2026, is gaining traction as a democratic framework rejecting hereditary rule.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed as Supreme National Security Council secretary post-January 2026 protests, reinforcing a security-first approach via IRGC ties.
Circumstantial evidence from March 20, 2026 suggests Mojtaba Khamenei remains incapacitated.
Deadly January 2026 protests led to Zolghadr's appointment, signaling reinforced crackdowns.
Kuwait arrested six Hezbollah-linked individuals plotting assassinations on March 26, 2026, amid regional fears tied to Iran conflict.
US strikes targeting Iranian sites including Tehran are degrading command, naval, and weapons capabilities as of March 26-28, 2026.
The conflict between US-Israel and Iran is characterized as a 'grinding middle phase' without decisive diplomacy as of March 28, 2026.
Iran has rejected US demands including Israel recognition as of March 29, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: U.S. and Israeli air campaign has executed approximately 9,000 strikes on Iranian military targets with three amphibious ready groups (carrying 7,000+ Marines) and 50,000+ U.S. troops positioned for potential ground operations; the military-dominated regime continues rejecting ceasefire terms and Israel recognition demands despite massive capability degradation, while VP Vance signals potential near-term exit pending Iranian concessions.
regime_fall.last_major_event: President Trump announced a 10-day suspension of strikes on Iranian power plants until April 6, 2026, following an Iranian request, while continuing operations elsewhere and expressing doubt on a peace deal dependent on Iranian leadership concessions.
